nodes.context: Paramilitary group composed of enemies of Pablo Escobar, waged war against Medellín Cartel in 1993.
Drug cartel led by Pablo Escobar, targeted by Los Pepes.
Drug cartel that financed Los Pepes.
National paramilitary alliance formed after Los Pepes, led by former Los Pepes leaders.
Marxist guerrilla group fought by AUC.
Criminal organization led by Diego Murillo Bejarano, former Los Pepes member.
Colombian law enforcement agency that had ties with Los Pepes.
Special police unit that cooperated with Los Pepes against Escobar.

edges.evidence_quote: They waged a small-scale war against the Medellín Cartel in 1993
edges.context: Los Pepes fought a war against the Medellín Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: The group was financed by the Cali Cartel
edges.context: Cali Cartel provided financial support to Los Pepes.

edges.evidence_quote: several of their leaders eventually went on to become leaders of a national paramilitary alliance in Colombia, the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC)
edges.context: Former Los Pepes leaders became leaders of AUC.

edges.evidence_quote: the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC), a paramilitary death squad that was formed not only for the purpose of fighting the Cartel but also the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC)
Another member of Los Pepes, Diego "Don Berna" Murillo Bejarano eventually became Inspector General of the AUC, as well as an important drug trafficker with Oficina de Envigado.
edges.context: A former Los Pepes member became a leader in Oficina de Envigado.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Cali Cartel, National Police of Colombia, Search Bloc
edges.context: Los Pepes had an alliance with the National Police of Colombia.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Cali Cartel, National Police of Colombia, Search Bloc
edges.context: Los Pepes had an alliance with the Search Bloc.
